chapter_title.en: Chapter on Selling in the Shade

text: : كُنْتُ أَبِيعُ اَلسَّابِرِيَّ فِي اَلظِّلاَلِ فَمَرَّ بِي أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ اَلْأَوَّلُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَاكِباً فَقَالَ لِي "يَا هِشَامُ إِنَّ اَلْبَيْعَ فِي اَلظِّلاَلِ غِشٌّ وَ اَلْغِشُّ لاَ يَحِلُّ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith. - Hisham ibn Al-Hakam narrated:
"I used to sell Sabiri (a type of cloth) in the shade, and Abu Al-Hasan the First (as) passed by me while riding.
Imam (as) said to me: 'O Hisham, selling in the shade is deceit, and deceit is not permissible.'"
